items.prompt: In ls -l, the first character - means…
items.explain: A leading dash marks a regular file.

items.prompt: In ls -l, the first character l means…
items.explain: Letter l marks a symbolic link.

items.prompt: readlink prints the target path of a symlink even if the target is missing.
items.explain: Broken links still store a target string; readlink shows it.

choices: Deletes only the link name, not the target file
Always deletes the target file too
Converts the link into a directory
items.explain: Removing a symlink removes the name; the target stays unless removed separately.
